Output.Newline TYPE: string/null VERSION: 2.0.1 DEFAULT: NULL --DESCRIPTION-- <p> Newline string to format final output with. If left null, HTML Purifier will auto-detect the default newline type of the system and use that; you can manually override it here. Remember, \r\n is Windows, \r is Mac, and \n is Unix. </p> --# vim: et sw=4 sts=4
